title: Tangy Australian Goose Skewers
description: A tangy and silky monsoon Australian dish featuring goose fat, this recipe is perfect for a unique dinner experience. It is shojin-friendly and cooked to perfection on an iron-teppan.
instructions: Preheat the iron-teppan to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, rice vinegar, garlic, and ginger to make the marinade.
Add the goose breast pieces to the marinade and toss to coat.
Thread the marinated goose breast pieces onto skewers.
Brush the iron-teppan with goose fat and cook the skewers for 5-6 minutes per side.
Brush the skewers with sesame oil during the last minute of cooking.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with sliced green onions and sesame seeds.
Let the skewers rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
